title: Design Manager, Project Management
description:
Design Project Manager - Bringing Creativity to Life with Efficiency


We are seeking a passionate and experienced Design Project Manager to join our team and lead the successful execution of exciting design projects from inception to completion. You will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the project, from understanding client needs to managing delivery within budget and timeframe.


A Day in the Life:

  • Client Relationship Management:
  1. Collaborate with clients to clearly define their design aspirations and translate them into a formal design brief.
  2. Facilitate seamless communication between the client and internal design team, ensuring client expectations are met and exceeded.
  3. Proactively manage client expectations and navigate through any changes or challenges efficiently.
  • Project Planning & Execution:
  1. Develop comprehensive project plans using MS Project or similar project management software, outlining key milestones, timelines, and resource allocation.
  2. Conduct regular meetings with client and internal teams to ensure clear communication, address design queries, and maintain project momentum.
  3. Coordinate effectively with internal designers, landlords, architects, engineers, and other stakeholders involved in the project.
  • Procurement & Tendering:
  1. Identify and recommend qualified consultants to join the project team through a defined RFP process.
  2. Assist the client in the tendering process, responding to technical queries, and clarifying project requirements.
  3. Evaluate tender submissions, providing well-informed recommendations to the client for informed decision-making.
  • Construction & Handover:
  1. Facilitate regular stakeholder meetings throughout the construction phase, ensuring open communication and alignment.
  2. Document meeting minutes for future reference and action tracking.
  3. Manage client change requests, translating them into clear instructions for the project team.
  4. Conduct regular site visits to monitor progress, oversee quality control, and address any logistical concerns.
  5. Manage testing and commissioning procedures for project readiness.
  6. Coordinate final inspections with landlord, consultants and contractors to ensure project compliance.
  7. Prepare punch-lists and diligently follow up with contractors to rectify deficiencies.
  8. Manage project close-out, including final accounts, handover documentation (as-built drawings, O&M manuals), and training sessions.
  9. Oversee reinstatement and move management activities, ensuring a smooth transition to project completion.

What We Are Looking For:

  • Minimum of 5 years of experience managing design projects in the Retail/Corporate industry. Junior candidates with demonstrably strong skills and relevant experience in design management may be considered.
  • Proven track record of successfully delivering complex design projects on time and within budget.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills in both written and verbal formats.
  • Strong organizational and time management abilities.
  • Proven ability to prioritize tasks, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and adapt to changing circumstances while maintaining deadlines.
  • Proficiency in design visualization software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit), project management software (e.g., MS Project, Primavera), and communication tools (e.g., Microsoft Office Suite). Knowledge of BIM (Building Information Modelling) systems a plus.
  • Experience with design procurement and tendering processes.
  • Keen understanding of design constructability, able to identify potential constructability issues early in the design process and collaborate with designers to find solutions. Experience working with contractors and understanding how design decisions impact buildability and cost.
  • Working knowledge of construction methods, materials, and regulations relevant to the industry. Ability to oversee construction activities, identify potential quality issues, and implement corrective actions promptly.
  • Demonstrated knowledge of project management best practices and a commitment to sustainability. Relevant certifications (e.g., PMP, LEED, etc.) are highly valued.

postedCompany.description:
Established in 1976, Kingsmen Design is the powerhouse behind the innovative design solutions for many globally recognized brands.

Our approach goes beyond aesthetics. We believe in design that fosters collaboration, communicates strategies, and inspires our people to achieve greatness. This philosophy is evident in our company culture, where creative thinking is embedded in everything we do.

With over 45 years of experience, Kingsmen Design has a proven track record of delivering exceptional design solutions that make a difference. We partner with clients to understand their unique needs and translate them into spaces that are not only functional and beautiful, but also create meaningful connections with users.
